desc: MSTR, COIN Rally as Bitcoin Price Climbs Above $73,000 · Freedom Is Paying Off for This Indie ETF · SSR Mining double upgraded to Buy at BofA after Copler mine sale
telegramText: EVENING WRAP — Mar 4, 2026 Markets reflected a tug-of-war between geopolitical risk-off sentiment and institutional capital flows, with Middle East tensions supporting oil and gold while crypto saw institutional buying overcome ETF outflows. The stagflation narrative strengthened as energy prices threatened to reignite inflation concerns despite mixed equity performance. CLOSING PRICES BTC: $73,726 · ETH: $2,181 · SOL: $93.5 · GOLD: $5,139 · SPY: $686.86 (+0.1%) · QQQ: $612.25 (+0.7%) · VIX: $20.56 (-12.8%) · TLT: $89.29 (-0.4%) · DXY: $98.83 (-0.2%) · OIL: $74.58 (+0.0%) EVENING NEWS 🟢 MSTR, COIN Rally as Bitcoin Price Climbs Above $73,000 🟢 Freedom Is Paying Off for This Indie ETF 🟢 SSR Mining double upgraded to Buy at BofA after Copler mine sale 🟢 Dogecoin Pumps as Bitcoin Pops, Reversing Recent DOGE Losing Streak 🔴 Owner of collapsed Brazilian bank arrested for second time 🟢 Fed’s Beige Book Shows Weaker Consumer Is Weighing on Sales SCENARIO SHIFTS ↑ Stagflation: Oil price rise and inflation fears from Middle East conflict are fueling stagflation. REGIME CHECK 🟢 Risk-On · Neutral · STAGFLATION · Gauge: 52.5/100 UPCOMING — HIGH IMPACT 🇺🇸 University of Michigan Consumer Confidence Index (Preliminary)(Mar) — Mar 13, 02:00 PM UTC (prev: 56.6) 🇺🇸 Personal consumption expenditure (MoM)(Jan) — Mar 13, 12:30 PM UTC (prev: 0.4%) 🇺🇸 Personal consumption expenditure price index (MoM)(Jan) — Mar 13, 12:30 PM UTC (prev: 0.4%) 🇺🇸 Personal income (MoM)(Jan) — Mar 13, 12:30 PM UTC (prev: 0.3%) 🌍 Caixin Services Purchasing Managers Index(Feb) — Mar 13, 02:06 AM UTC (prev: 52.3) 🇺🇸 Producer Price Index (YoY)(Feb) — Mar 12, 12:30 PM UTC (prev: 2.9%) CONTRARIAN • Bitcoin will rally to $80k as institutional inflows continue to outweigh ETF sell pressure. • Oil will decline as supply disruptions resolve and geopolitical tensions ease. agentcanary.ai | Not financial advice
